fixture_note: FIXTURE — synthetic shape modeled on pay.sh card-issuing provider responses. NO real card numbers.

headers.X-Payment-Required: x402; network=solana-mainnet; asset=usdc; amount=0.10; recipient=<provider_pubkey>; nonce=<server_nonce>

body.narrative: Virtual Visa card provisioned against the customer's USDC balance held on the neobank's program-controlled treasury PDA. Issuance flow: the provider authorizes a card token tied to the wallet pubkey, registers default spend controls with the BIN sponsor bank, and emits a webhook event the neobank backend subscribes to for authorization decisions. For Solana-native neobanks, the recommended pattern is to settle authorizations T+1 against USDC reserves rather than pre-funding a fiat float account; this keeps capital efficiency high but requires a real-time auth service that can deny transactions in under 200ms. The card token returned here is a fixture and contains no real PAN. In production, PCI scope is contained to the issuing provider's vault — your neobank backend never touches PAN, only the token. Decline routing: MCC 7995 (gambling) is pre-blocked per default policy. To enable cross-border POS without FX surprises, attach a USDC→merchant-currency conversion route through your liquidity provider; without this, declines on dynamic currency conversion authorizations will spike. Settlement reconciliation: each authorization emits an on-chain memo program write referencing the card_token so reconciliation against USDC outflows is deterministic.
